Class.title: Class
Class.description: Represents a specific class or group of students within the school, such as '10 IPA 1' or 'Class 5A'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Class entity.

name.description: The official name of the class (e.g., '10 IPA 1', 'Class 5A').

gradeLevel.description: The academic grade level this class belongs to (e.g., '10' for tenth grade, '5' for fifth grade).

homeroomTeacherId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the Teacher assigned as the homeroom teacher for this class. (Relationship: Class N:1 Teacher)

Teacher.title: Teacher
Teacher.description: Represents a teacher employed at the school, including their contact and login identification information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Teacher entity.

name.description: The full name of the teacher.

email.description: The email address of the teacher, which is used as their login identifier. This email is managed by the admin.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the teacher.

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student enrolled in the school, including personal details, parent contact information, and their current class enrollment.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

nis.description: The Nomor Induk Siswa (Student ID Number), a unique identifier for the student. This NIS is also used by parents as their login identifier.

name.description: The full name of the student.

address.description: The residential address of the student.

parentName.description: The full name of the student's primary parent or guardian.

parentPhoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the student's primary parent or guardian.

classId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the Class the student is currently enrolled in. (Relationship: Student N:1 Class)

SPPPayment.title: SPPPayment
SPPPayment.description: Records monthly SPP (Sumbangan Pembinaan Pendidikan - educational contribution) payments made by or on behalf of students.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SPPPayment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the Student for whom the payment is made. (Relationship: SPPPayment N:1 Student)

classId.description: Reference to the unique identifier of the Class the student was associated with at the time the payment record was created. This helps with historical context and UI filtering for payment tables. (Relationship: SPPPayment N:1 Class)

month.description: The month (1-12, e.g., 1 for January) for which the SPP payment is due.

year.description: The year (e.g., 2023) for which the SPP payment is due.

amountDue.description: The total expected amount of SPP due for this specific month.

amountPaid.description: The actual amount of SPP paid for this month.

paymentDate.description: The date when the payment was recorded or made.

status.description: The current status of the payment (e.g., 'Paid', 'Unpaid', 'Partial').

notes.description: Any additional notes or remarks about the payment.

definition.description: Collection of all classes. Each class document identifies its homeroom teacher via `homeroomTeacherId`.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific class.

definition.description: Collection of all teacher profiles. The `teacherId` in the path matches the `id` field within the document. Admin manages these records.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific teacher.

definition.description: Collection of all student records. The `studentId` in the path matches the `id` field within the document. Includes `classId` for enrollment. Admin manages these records.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific student.

definition.description: Records of SPP payments. Each document includes `studentId`, `classId`, and a denormalized `homeroomTeacherId` (copied from the associated Class document at creation/update) to enable Authorization Independence and QAPs for teachers and parents. The `SPPPayment` entity schema should implicitly include `homeroomTeacherId` for this denormalization.

params.name: sppPaymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific SPP payment record.

definition.description: Collection for admin role assignments. The existence of a document at this path signifies the associated `uid` has admin privileges. `adminUid` maps directly to `request.auth.uid`. The document content could be minimal, e.g., `{"uid": "string"}`.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user with admin privileges.

definition.description: Collection for teacher role assignments. Links an authenticated `uid` (`teacherUid`) to a specific `Teacher` entity via a `teacherId` field within the document. Used for teacher-specific authorization. The document content should be `{"uid": "string", "teacherId": "string"}`.

params.name: teacherUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user with teacher privileges.

definition.description: Collection for parent role assignments. Links an authenticated `uid` (`parentUid`) to a specific `Student` entity via a `studentId` field within the document. Used for parent-specific authorization. The document content should be `{"uid": "string", "studentId": "string"}` (where `studentId` is the Student's `id` or `nis`).

params.name: parentUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the user with parent privileges.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and highly debuggable, strictly adhering to the core design principles. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization (CRITICAL):**

The most critical aspect of this design is how `SPPPayment` documents are structured to enable authorization independence. Each `SPPPayment` document will contain `studentId`, `classId`, and crucially, a denormalized `homeroomTeacherId`. This `homeroomTeacherId` is copied from the `Class` document associated with the student at the time the payment record is created. This denormalization eliminates the need for security rules to perform `get()` operations on the parent `Class` document to determine a teacher's authorization to manage or view payments. For example, a teacher's rule for `SPPPayment` can simply check `request.auth.teacherId == resource.data.homeroomTeacherId`, without additional database reads. Similarly, parents can access their own child's payments via `request.auth.studentId == resource.data.studentId`.

User roles (Admin, Teacher, Parent) are managed through dedicated top-level collections: `/roles_admin/{adminUid}`, `/roles_teacher/{teacherUid}`, and `/roles_parent/{parentUid}`. These collections adhere to the 'Existence over Content' principle for basic role checks, simplifying security rules significantly. For Teachers and Parents, these role documents also contain a direct reference to their associated `teacherId` or `studentId` respectively (e.g., `/roles_teacher/{uid}` contains `teacherId`). This allows rules to retrieve the user's specific entity ID once at the beginning of a rule evaluation, without requiring further `get()` calls for most data access checks.

**Support for Querying and Authorization Predicates (QAPs):**

1.  **Admin:** Admin users, identified by their `uid` existing in `/roles_admin/{adminUid}`, will have full read/write access across all collections. This enables them to `list` all `Class`, `Teacher`, `Student`, and `SPPPayment` documents without filtering limitations.

2.  **Teacher:** Teachers, identified by their `uid` in `/roles_teacher/{teacherUid}` and associated `teacherId` (which matches `Teacher.id`), can perform the following secure `list` operations:
    *   **Classes:** Teachers can list classes where their `teacherId` matches `homeroomTeacherId` in the `Class` document. This allows filtering for their assigned classes.
    *   **Students:** Teachers can list `Student` documents where `classId` matches the `id` of a class they homeroom. Rules would resolve the teacher's `teacherId` from their role document, then query `Class` documents for `homeroomTeacherId == teacherId`, and finally query `Student` documents for `classId` within that set. While this requires intermediate steps, the `SPPPayment` structure directly supports QAPs.
    *   **SPP Payments:** Teachers can list `SPPPayment` documents by querying `homeroomTeacherId == request.auth.teacherId`. This is a direct, efficient query, completely independent of `get()` operations on other documents for payment records, thus fulfilling the QAP requirement for teachers.

3.  **Parent:** Parents, identified by their `uid` in `/roles_parent/{parentUid}` and associated `studentId` (which matches `Student.id` or `Student.nis`), can securely `list` and view data pertinent to their child:
    *   **Student Data:** Parents can read their specific `Student` document by checking `request.auth.studentId == resource.data.id`.
    *   **SPP Payments:** Parents can list `SPPPayment` documents by querying `studentId == request.auth.studentId`. This is also a direct and efficient query, independent of additional `get()` calls, fulfilling the QAP requirement for parents.

**Structural Segregation and Consistency:**

Each collection (e.g., `/classes`, `/teachers`, `/students`, `/sppPayments`, `/roles_admin`, `/roles_teacher`, `/roles_parent`) maintains a homogeneous security posture. For instance, all documents within `/sppPayments` have the same access requirements based on `studentId` and `homeroomTeacherId`. This significantly simplifies security rules, making them clearer and less prone to errors. Wildcards are descriptive (e.g., `{classId}`, `{studentId}`), and authorization fields are consistently named (`homeroomTeacherId`, `studentId`). This design prioritizes explicit structure over complex rule logic, leading to a more robust and maintainable system.
